title: centaurea coerulescens subsp. leucophaea
intro: Centaurea coerulescens subsp. leucophaea, also known as Mountain Knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant's medicinal properties can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
